nodes.name: OrderFlowImbalanceEvaluation
nodes.description: Computes the order flow imbalance signal for the current observation relative to the previous observation as a pure, stateless transformation.

inputs.name: row
inputs.constraints: Must contain the fields required for OFI computation.

inputs.name: prev_row
inputs.constraints: Represents the immediately preceding row; schema-compatible with row.

outputs.name: ofi_value
outputs.constraints: Deterministic scalar derived only from row and prev_row.

nodes.type_signature: (row: record-like market data row, prev_row: record-like market data row) -> float
nodes.conceptual_summary: BidirectionalStateDeltaImbalance. Maps two consecutive, schema-compatible records into a single signed scalar by conditionally differencing paired quantities from two opposing channels. For each channel, the transform applies direction-gated arithmetic (based on whether reference levels moved up, down, or stayed equal) and then aggregates the channel contributions into a net imbalance score. The result is a first-order temporal contrast that encodes which side gained relative influence between the two observations.. Applications: Computing directional load pressure in distributed systems from consecutive inbound vs outbound queue-depth snapshots., Estimating net membrane transport bias in cellular assays from sequential concentrations on two opposing sides of a boundary., Deriving airflow dominance shifts in HVAC diagnostics from successive supply-side and return-side sensor states., Quantifying bidirectional migration imbalance in ecological monitoring from consecutive counts across two connected habitats.
